notes: small direct-drive (ungeared) brushless propulsion motor — unlike n20-motor-6v, there's no integrated gearbox, so it trades torque for RPM: lower stall torque, much higher no-load speed. A real build would usually still gear or belt-reduce this for a sensible wheel speed; that reduction isn't modelled here, just the motor's own electrical draw.
description: A small brushless drive motor — an alternative to a brushed N20 gearmotor for propulsion: better power-to-weight, needs its own brushless ESC (not the brushed dual-ESC), and no gearbox of its own.
